text: Iteration 2 fixes all 4 critical regressions from iteration 1 (Header parse error, PointsHistory/CategoryPieChart Hooks violations, ProtectedRoute useMemo deps). Performance optimizations across 33 files are correct and well-implemented. However, a new critical security issue was introduced in ProtectedRoute (substring role matching bypass), and the TierPathVisualizer progress bar bug from iteration 1 remains unfixed. Several pre-existing important/minor items from prior audits are also still present.

reasoning: Two critical issues block merge: (1) ProtectedRoute.tsx uses String.includes() instead of Array.includes() — ORGANIZATION_ADMIN can bypass ADMIN route restrictions via substring match; (2) TierPathVisualizer.tsx missing right-0 on absolutely-positioned progress bar makes it invisible. Both are straightforward to fix.

message: Correctly fixed the critical parse-error regression from iteration 1. Changed '' : 'hidden' (empty string) to 'block' : 'hidden', and confirmed no remaining extra . Mobile menu toggles correctly.
Added useCallback for toggle function with [setTheme, resolvedTheme] deps. Addresses the audit's minor finding and follows best practices for event handler stability.
Removed the IIFE wrapper around date formatting — simplified to direct new Date(earnedAt).toLocaleDateString() expression. The guard (earned && earnedAt) ensures safety from null.
Added will-change-transform to decorative blur divs. Promotes to GPU compositor layer, mitigating the expensive blur+backdrop-filter compositing on low-end devices.
Computes currentTier once before the map loop (O(n)) instead of O(n²) levels.find() inside levels.map(). Correct performance improvement.
Correctly replaced blanket watch() with targeted watch('frequency'), watch('daysOfWeek'), etc. Individual field subscriptions prevent re-render on every unrelated field change.
VolunteerRow is properly extracted as a memo() component with stable callback props. Prevents re-render of unchanged rows on any single interaction.
useMemo for formatted date data correctly placed BEFORE early returns (unlike the iteration 1 regression). Deps [data] are correct.
useMemo for chartData correctly placed before the early return (unlike the iteration 1 regression). Deps [data] are correct.

rolesKey.includes(user.role) uses String.prototype.includes(), which does SUBSTRING matching instead of exact matching. Since UserRole includes both 'ADMIN' and 'ORGANIZATION_ADMIN', the string 'ORGANIZATION_ADMIN' contains 'ADMIN' as a substring, so an ORGANIZATION_ADMIN user can bypass ADMIN-only route restrictions. This is a security regression introduced in iteration 2.
suggestion: Use roleList.split(',').includes(user.role) instead: const roleList = allowedRoles.join(','); const roles = roleList.split(','); if (!roles.includes(user.role)) { ... } — this preserves the stable-string-key approach for useEffect deps while using exact Array.includes() matching.

message: Absolutely-positioned progress fill div has left:0 but no explicit width or right:0. An absolutely-positioned contentless <div> has width 0, so transform: scaleX(0.5) × 0px = 0px — the progress bar is invisible regardless of completionPct. This regression was introduced when width: ${completionPct}% was replaced with transform: scaleX().
suggestion: Add right-0 so the div stretches the full container width before scaleX reduces it: className="absolute top-1/2 left-0 right-0 h-1 -translate-y-1/2 bg-gradient-to-r from-brand-primary to-brand-secondary rounded-full origin-left transition-transform duration-700"

message: TypeScript cast as EventSeriesFormData suppresses 8 missing fields (title, description, startTime, endTime, venue, isVirtual, meetingLink, capacity) from the partial object passed to calculatePreviewDates. If that function is later modified to access these fields, the bug will be silent until runtime.
suggestion: Use Pick<EventSeriesFormData, 'frequency' | 'daysOfWeek' | 'interval' | 'firstEventDate' | 'endType' | 'maxOccurrences' | 'endDate'> instead of the blanket cast.

message: useEffect dependency [volunteers.length] means if an existing volunteer's properties change (e.g. checkedInAt updated via sync) without the array length changing, local state won't resync. Intentional trade-off for performance, but could cause stale UI after data refetch.
suggestion: Document this design choice clearly, or use a hash of volunteer IDs if mutations need to be reflected.

message: Still uses namespace import import * as Sentry from '@sentry/nextjs' which prevents tree-shaking. Only Sentry.captureException and Sentry.withScope (from handleApprove error path) are used.
suggestion: Replace with import { captureException } from '@sentry/nextjs' for consistency with the fix already applied in ProofUploadForm.tsx.

message: Still uses namespace import import * as Sentry which prevents tree-shaking of unused Sentry exports.
Still uses namespace import import * as Sentry which prevents tree-shaking, though only captureException is used.

onChange prop passed to RichTextEditor is an inline arrow function (html) => setValue('content', html), creating a new reference every render. This makes the useCallback in RichTextEditor.tsx:43 ineffective since handleUpdate depends on [onChange].
suggestion: Wrap the onChange prop in useCallback with stable dependencies.

message: htmlFor="recurring-toggle-active" targets a <div>, not a form element. The label click will not activate the toggle. Pre-existing accessibility issue, not a regression.
suggestion: Use a proper checkbox/switch input element or apply role="switch" with aria-labelledby.
